§6Bismuth§f

§aThe post-RBMK gate element — essential for advanced chipsets, fusion reactors, and DFCs!§f

§bBismuth§f is an extremely rare heavy element (atomic number 83) commonly used to make versatile chipsets and other late-tier items. It is a §epost-RBMK§f material.

§6Production§f:
§b• Nuclear Waste Decay§f — ~15% bismuth from most short/long-lived waste decay chains
  • Decayed HEP-241 fuel waste: §e55%§f bismuth
  • Neptunium fuel: §e0%§f bismuth (exception)
§b• Bismuth Zirconium Fast Breeders§f — §e43.3%§f yield when fully depleted; initial investment 8 nuggets/rod, expected return ~20-21 nuggets
§b• Lead-209 Decay§f — from spent low-enriched australium fuel
§b• Bedrock Ore§f — process with high-performance solvent

§6Key Uses§f:
§b• Versatile Chipsets§f — used in control cores of the Fusion Reactor and DFC
§b• Vacuum Refinery§f and §bHydrotreater§f
§b• Moderated RBMK Fuel§f and §bControl Rods§f — more compact designs for slow neutron fuels
§b• SiOX Cancer Medication§f — eliminates lung diseases
§b• Tier 5 Anvil§f
§b• Bismuth Pickaxe§f

§eTip§f: Bismuth is the §egate§f between the RBMK age and end-game. Set up BiZFB breeders in your RBMK ASAP once you have a stable reactor running!
